title: Score Reconciliation, Waves, Safe Spawns, Pacing, and Difficulty Options · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers score reconciliation, waves, safe spawns, pacing, and difficulty options into a changed fair progression and difficulty model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of fair progression and difficulty model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit the…
objectives: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
Advance only when active, queued, and pending-fragment counts satisfy a declared completion invariant.
Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Score Ledger
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 8781 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Score Ledger.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Score Ledger, keep Score Ledger connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Difficulty Access
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-access-1a6vps" that uses case 7930 to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Difficulty Access.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Difficulty Access into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Difficulty Access, keep Difficulty Access connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-access-1a6vps
# Competency: asteroids-difficulty-access.
# Case 7930: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_difficulty_access_1a6vps(destroyed: int = 9, score_per_hit: int = 100, score: int = 900, wave: int = 3, safe_spawn: bool = True, difficulty_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1a6vps = "1a6vps-spv6a1-7930"
    assert evidence_variant_1a6vps
    evidence_axes_1a6vps = (
        "player1a6vps",
        "seed7930",
        "inputspv6a1",
        "frame1a6vps7930",
        "state79301a6vps",
        "playtestspv6a17930",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1a6vps)) == 6
    if destroyed < 0 or score_per_hit < 0 or wave < 1:
        return False, score, "progression-domain"
    if score != destroyed * score_per_hit:
        return False, score, "score-drift"
    if not safe_spawn:
        return False, wave, "unsafe-spawn"
    if not difficulty_bounded:
        return False, wave, "difficulty-runaway"
    assert score == destroyed * score_per_hit
    return True, wave, "fair-progression"
